title: Electronic signatures — control theme 3

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §11.200.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that FDA Part 11 §11.200 (Electronic signatures) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.17.8: Electronic signatures — control theme 3.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.17.8' running on LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
spl:
index=gxp sourcetype=okta:auth earliest=-30d application="ELN-Prod"
| eval uc_tag="22.17.8"
| where authenticationContext.credentialType!="FIDO2" AND authenticationContext.credentialType!="X509"
| stats count by user, authenticationContext.credentialType

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=okta:auth earliest=-30d application="ELN-Prod"
| eval uc_tag="22.17.8"
| where authenticationContext.credentialType!="FIDO2" AND authenticationContext.credentialType!="X509"
| stats count by user, authenticationContext.credentialType
```

Understanding this SPL

**ELN logins without FIDO2 or X.509 credential (21 CFR Part 11)** — Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.

Documented **Data sources**: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gxp; **sourcetype**: okta:auth. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gxp, sourcetype=okta:auth, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where authenticationContext.credentialType!="FIDO2" AND authenticationContext.credentialType!="X509"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, authenticationContext.credentialType** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.



Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the source-of-truth system (LIMS, MES, or ELN) and compare with the audit register. Confirm users, instruments, and timestamps align with controlled quality records for the same activity.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value
grandmaExplanation: We help you show that electronic signatures in your validated lab and manufacturing systems line up with how you said they would work, so food and drug inspections can trust the record.
